name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in analytical method development, materials characterization, and process development across small-molecule, polymer, and nanoparticle systems. Deep expertise in spectroscopy (FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR), chromatography (HPLC, LC-MS, GC-MS), thermal analysis (TGA, DSC), and statistical experimental design (DOE, SPC). Proven ability to solve complex analytical challenges from first principles, establish structure-property-processing relationships, and generate high-quality data to guide molecular and formulation decisions in cross-functional environments.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Leading chemical process development and optimization for novel bio-based polymer and nanoparticle systems, applying statistical experimental design to identify critical process parameters, improve yield and product quality, and establish reproducible process specifications for scale-up.

Developing and applying analytical methods including spectroscopy, chromatography, and thermal analysis to characterize polymer nanoparticles and establish structure-property relationships for drug delivery applications.

Designed and executed analytical characterization of 15+ organic and organometallic material variants using FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, cyclic voltammetry, NMR, and thermal analysis (TGA, DSC), identifying degradation pathways and establishing structure-property-processing relationships to guide molecular design decisions.

Developed stress test protocols and analytical methods from first principles to evaluate long-term stability under controlled electrochemical and optical conditions, generating high-quality datasets for cross-functional team review.

Co-led AFOSR-funded process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings, developing surface preparation protocols and CVD-based deposition strategies to achieve controlled film morphology and device performance.

Developed and optimized CVD-based polymer thin-film deposition processes using DOE strategies and in-situ QCM-D process monitoring, establishing process control parameters and characterizing film growth kinetics to improve coating reproducibility and uniformity.

Managed process development laboratory operations for a 15-member research group; implemented equipment tracking and calibration systems improving instrument uptime by 30%, developed SOPs and safety protocols, and coordinated cross-functional resource planning across multiple concurrent projects.

projects.title: Polymer Nanoparticle Analytical Development
projects.description: Leading analytical development for bio-based polymer nanoparticle systems, applying multi-technique characterization (spectroscopy, chromatography, thermal analysis, particle sizing) to support process development and formulation decisions for drug delivery applications.
projects.tech: Polymer nanoparticles, spectroscopy, thermal analysis, DOE, analytical method development

projects.title: Small Molecule Structural Elucidation
projects.description: Characterized organic and organometallic compounds using NMR, MS, FT-IR, UV-Vis, and electrochemical methods to determine molecular structure, degradation pathways, and stability profiles for 15+ novel material systems.
projects.tech: NMR, MS, FT-IR, UV-Vis, cyclic voltammetry, structural elucidation, degradation analysis

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558-5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048-200055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.

skills.Materials Characterization: TGA, DSC, cyclic voltammetry, nanoparticle characterization, polymer analysis, thin-film analysis, structure-property-processing relationships
skills.Statistical Analysis: DOE, Statistical Process Control (SPC, Coursera 2026), JMP (basic), Python (basic), Excel (advanced), statistical data analysis
skills.Materials Expertise: Small molecules, polymer systems, nanoparticles (polymer and lipid), organic thin films, surface functionalization
skills.Regulatory & Quality: cGMP awareness, ICH guidelines (familiarity), analytical control strategies, SOP development, technical documentation
skills.Project Management: Multi-project management, cross-functional collaboration, technical documentation, stakeholder communication
